response.justification: The candidate is a strong, seasoned generalist software engineer (15+ years across web, backend, and embedded, with solid TypeScript/Node.js and craftsmanship practices like TDD and CI/CD), but the role requires 8+ years building production-grade AI solutions and the resume shows only roughly one year of AI-adjacent work (MCP code generators and agentic-workflow tooling at re:cinq since 2025). There is no demonstrated experience with RAG architectures, vector databases, evaluation pipelines, LLM optimization, or multi-agent frameworks, which are core to this staff-level GenAI delivery role.
strengths: 15+ years of broad production software experience with TypeScript, Node.js, and cloud tooling (AWS CDK)
Recent hands-on work with MCP servers and AI-agent/developer tooling infrastructure
Proven mentorship and communication skills via workshops, conference talks, and courses
concerns: Falls far short of the required 8+ years building AI-driven solutions (~1 year of AI-related work)
No evidence of RAG, vector databases, LLM evaluation/observability, or multi-agent frameworks like LangGraph/CrewAI
Limited demonstrated customer-facing technical discovery or forward-deployed consulting experience at enterprise scale

key_factors.factor: Strong general engineering and MCP/agent tooling exposure
